s_per_epoch * approx_epochs)\\n\\n    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.1 * total_steps))\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(step_idx: int) -> float:\\n        s = step_idx + 1\\n        if s <= warmup_steps:\\n            return s / warmup_steps\\n        progress = (s - warmup_steps) / max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)\\n        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + math.cos(math.pi * progress))\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"With a fixed 256-step budget, the baseline effectively trains at a constant small LR and underuses the short horizon. A warmup+cosine per-step schedule improves optimization efficiency early while keeping updates stable, and enabling it is a low-complexity change with high expected gain in validation loss.\",\"secondary_modes\":[\"topk\"]}"}}
